title: [macOS][ARM] podman run does not show a prompt emulating Arch Linux (amd64)
body:
### Issue Description

On ARM macOS, `podman` now seems to hang emulating Arch Linux. And I can't ctrl-c.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman pull --arch amd64 archlinux2.
2. podman run -it --arch amd64 archlinux


### Describe the results you received

```
% podman run -it --arch amd64 archlinux
^C^C^C^C
```

(It hangs and I can't ctrl-c)

### Describe the results you expected

The container runs and a prompt is displayed. I used to be able to run Arch Linux containers using `podman`, but I don't know what version.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Happens always. Also in the previous release.

comments.body: I think this is an issue connected to reseta and kernel support, similar to the one in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26572. What do you think, @Luap99?

yes also see https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

5.6 now use qemu which may not work where rosetta might have used to work. Ultimately neither emulation works perfect and there isn't really anything we (Podman) can do abut it. Best to create a minimal reproducer and report such issues to qemu-user-static upstream.

Also ctrl-c send SIGINT which any application can choose to ignore, best to stop/kill the container from anther terminal via podman stop/kill.

Interestingly the container is starting, it's the container CMD of `bash` that isn't working correctly.

```
$ podman run -it --arch amd64 quay.io/archlinux/archlinux
echo test
test
blah
/usr/bin/bash: line 2: blah: command not found
```

The standard bash `ctrl-d` can exit the container.

Overriding the containers default CMD you can get it to work:
```
> podman run -it --arch amd64 quay.io/archlinux/archlinux bash -l -i
bash: /etc/machine-id: No such file or directory
[root@9a3eb34d1bc9 /]#
```

But this wonkness is not reproducible on an `amd` system so as @Luap99 and @Honny1 said it seems like an emulation issue. 

```
> podman run -it --arch amd64 quay.io/archlinux/archlinux bash -l -i
bash: /etc/machine-id: No such file or directory
[root@9a3eb34d1bc9 /]#
```

Thx for the sanity check, clarification, and workaround! I have updated the issue title accordingly.

It is definitely useful knowing that the container runs but the prompt is not showing. I did not think of that. I'll add ctrl-d to my list of things to do when a container hangs!

And yes, stopping the container works too.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This happens to the fedora image too:
